tasks.description: Task count badges on step canvas nodes (e.g. '2/5') + section-level task rollup in section detail panel. Final sub-task [3/3] of the tasks system feature.
acceptance_criteria: Tasks API GET route accepts optional step_id — when omitted, returns all tasks for the workspace
New client function fetchAllTasks(workspaceId) returns Task[] for entire workspace
TaskCountsContext created in canvas.ts — Map<string, { completed: number; total: number }> keyed by step_id
canvas-view.tsx fetches all workspace tasks on mount, builds TaskCountsContext map, wraps ReactFlow in provider (mirrors CommentCountsContext pattern)
step-node.tsx consumes TaskCountsContext and displays 'completed/total' badge (e.g. '2/5') — only when total > 0, positioned near comment badge area
section-detail-panel.tsx shows task rollup: for each step in the section, display step name + task progress (completed/total). Summary at top: total completed / total tasks across all steps

similar_files: src/types/canvas.ts — CommentCountsContext (line 8) is the exact pattern to replicate for TaskCountsContext
src/components/canvas/step-node.tsx — comment count badge (lines 114-123) is the pattern for task count badge placement
src/app/(app)/w/[workspaceId]/[tabId]/canvas-view.tsx — comment counts fetch+provide (lines 119-136) is the pattern for task counts
patterns_to_follow: CommentCountsContext: createContext → canvas-view fetches all comments once → builds Map → wraps ReactFlow in Provider → node components useContext
TaskCountsContext value type is Map<string, { completed: number; total: number }> (richer than comment counts which is just number)
Badge positioning: comment badge is absolute bottom-1 right-1. Task badge should use a different position or inline placement to avoid overlap
Section rollup: section-detail-panel already receives steps prop — can consume TaskCountsContext to show per-step progress
learnings_relevant: apiFetch unwraps envelope — returns T directly, do NOT add nested property access
React state immutability — use [...arr].sort() not arr.sort()
React 19 useRef requires initial value: useRef(null)
affected_urls: /w/[workspaceId]/[tabId] — process canvas view (step nodes show task badges)
/w/[workspaceId]/[tabId] — section detail panel (rollup when section selected)

tasks.special_instructions: The tasks API GET route currently REQUIRES step_id. Make it optional: when step_id is omitted, query all tasks for the workspace_id. This enables workspace-wide task fetching for the canvas badge context. Add a new fetchAllTasks(workspaceId) client function — do NOT modify the existing fetchTasks(workspaceId, stepId) signature since TaskPanel depends on it. For the task count badge on step-node, use ListTodo or CheckSquare icon (import from lucide-react). Position it to avoid overlap with the existing comment count badge — consider placing it inline in the status row or as a separate bottom-left badge. The shared_files.owner grants permission to modify client.ts.

tasks.description: Fix flow-canvas handleKeyDown useCallback with incomplete dependency array — add handleAddStep and handleAddSection to deps.
acceptance_criteria: handleKeyDown useCallback dependency array includes handleAddStep and handleAddSection
handleAddStep and handleAddSection are wrapped in useCallback (if not already) to prevent unnecessary re-renders
No behavioral change — keyboard shortcuts continue to work identically

patterns_to_follow: React hooks exhaustive-deps: all variables referenced inside useCallback must be in the dependency array
If handleAddStep/handleAddSection reference props, wrap them in useCallback with those props as deps
learnings_relevant: React 19 useRef requires initial value: useRef(null)

tasks.special_instructions: This is a code quality fix. Read flow-canvas.tsx fully before making changes. The handleKeyDown useCallback references handleAddStep and handleAddSection but doesn't list them in deps. Wrap those handlers in useCallback if they aren't already, then add them to handleKeyDown's dep array. Verify no infinite re-render loop — if the handlers depend on frequently-changing state, consider using refs instead.

shared_files.note: Slot 1 (#FEAT-046) may add fetchAllTasks function to src/lib/api/client.ts. Slot 2 must NOT modify client.ts.

testing_plan.regression_reason: Last regression was iter 75 (32/32 PASS). Risk score 4 from iter 77 suggests regression — scheduled for iter 79 after FEAT-046 is fully complete.

warnings_added: Risk score 4 from iter 77 (shared canvas components touched). Regression recommended for iter 79.
